# traceforce_source_apps (Data Source)

Retrieves information about existing Traceforce source apps.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Get all source apps
data "traceforce_source_apps" "all" {}

# Filter source apps by datalake
data "traceforce_source_apps" "datalake_apps" {
  filter = {
    datalake_id = traceforce_datalake.analytics.id
  }
}

# Filter by project (get all source apps in a project)
data "traceforce_source_apps" "project_apps" {
  filter = {
    project_id = traceforce_project.production.id
  }
}

# Use source app data for reference
output "active_source_apps" {
  value = [
    for app in data.traceforce_source_apps.all.source_apps : app
    if app.status == "Connected"
  ]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `filter` (Object) Filter criteria for source apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--filter))

### Read-Only

- `source_apps` (List of Object) List of source apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--source_apps))

<a id="nestedatt--filter"></a>
### Nested Schema for `filter`

Optional:

- `datalake_id` (String) Filter by datalake ID
- `project_id` (String) Filter by project ID (via datalake relationship)
- `status` (String) Filter by source app status
- `type` (String) Filter by source app type

<a id="nestedatt--source_apps"></a>
### Nested Schema for `source_apps`

Read-Only:

- `id` (String) System generated unique identifier
- `name` (String) Source app name
- `datalake_id` (String) ID of the datalake this source app feeds data to
- `type` (String) Source app type
- `status` (String) Current source app status
- `created_at` (String) Creation timestamp
- `updated_at` (String) Last update timestamp
